If the card is not enrolled, this means that either the bank that issued the card is not yet supporting 3D Secure or it means that the cardholder has not yet been registered for the service. If however the card is enrolled, the MPI will redirect the cardholder to the 3D Secure authentication web page for the issuing bank; the cardholder will then identify himself.

When a payment request arrives at the merchant or payment gateway, the Merchant Plug In (MPI) component is activated. The MPI talks to Payment scheme (Visa or MasterCard) to check if the card is enrolled for 3D Secure.

Enrolment Enquiry When a payment request arrives at the merchant or payment gateway, the Merchant Plug In (MPI) component is activated. The MPI talks to Payment scheme (Visa or MasterCard) to check if the card is enrolled for 3D Secure. End User Identification If the card is not enrolled, this means that either the bank that issued the card is not yet supporting 3D Secure or it means that the cardholder has not yet been registered for the service. If however the card is enrolled, the MPI will redirect the cardholder to the 3D Secure authentication web page for the issuing bank; the cardholder will then identify himself. Transaction Authorisation The MPI will evaluate the reply from the bank and, if successful, allow the transaction to proceed for authorisation. The transaction could still fail for lack of funds or other reasons but is more likely to be approved because of the authentication.

3D Secure, such as Verified by Visa and MasterCard SecureCode, allows transactions to be conducted in safety online, greatly reducing the risk of fraud and chargebacks. The process allows the cardholder domain, interoperability domain and merchant domain (3 Domains or 3D) to securely communicate with each other and allow the authentication of the cardholder transaction.

AC Assure is looking for a computer security professional to join the team to perform logical and physical security assessments and consultancy. The companyâs core business is providing information security services for the financial and banking sector, mainly across Europe. This post will focus primarily on performing Payment Card Industry (PCI) Card Production audits. Main Responsibilities â¢ Conduct financial scheme PCI Card Production security assessments. â¢ Presentation of findings to client at senior management level. â¢ Produce compliance reports for clients and regulatory bodies. â¢ Development of UK based security consultancy and Cyber Essential audits. â¢ Support the development of new business services. Experience & Qualifications Candidates must be able to demonstrate at least 3 years practical experience within the following areas: â¢ Cryptography & key management â¢ Physical security â¢ Data security â¢ Network & system security â¢ Database security â¢ Application security Candidates should have a computer related degree or have a recognised certification such as CISSP, CISA or CISM. Any candidates with experience of the PCI Card Production requirements will have a distinct advantage as this will form the core element of this role. It would be advantageous for candidates to also have experience in computer forensics, penetration testing, smartcard technologies, EMV, mobile OTA, NFC and HCE technologies. Practical experience of governance and compliance services, such as ISO/IEC 27000 series and PCI-DSS, is also desirable. Personal Skills You must be professional, organised, self-motivated, and technically competent. You display excellent written and verbal communication, are a quick learner and possess a real desire to contribute to a developing small company. Further Details The position will be offered on a permanent basis, subject to the successful completion of a probationary period and a third party assessment, for which full training will be provided. A degree of flexibility is required from staff due to the foreign travel as this could account for up to 50% of your working time. Although the company offices are based in Cheshire, the company operates a remote and home working option so it not necessary to be based in the Northwest. The job will initially attract a salary range of £35,000 to £50,000 along with a bonus scheme related to performance and contribution to business development. Interested? If you feel you can bring something extra to our team, please contact us (careers@acassure.com) with your CV and covering letter detailing your suitability.

section: What are the benefits of Cyber Essentials certification?
text: Provides cost-effective, basic cyber security for organizations of all sizes Demonstrates that an organization meets one of the eligibility requirements when bidding for UK Government contracts Can reduce the risk of prevalent cyber-attacks on an organization Differentiate yourself from your competitors by demonstrating that you take cyber security seriously How can we help? As Approved Cyber Essential (ACE) consultants we are in an ideal position get assist you in completing your questionnaire or preparing for your formal assessment. If you'd like to know more about the first steps to improving your cyber security please get in touch with us using the contact form, or give us a call on +44 (0) 8443 760400.

The Cyber Essentials scheme has been developed by Government and industry to fulfil two main functions. The first of these is to provide a framework of basic controls that all organisations can implement that will help them to mitigate the risks from common internet based threats. The core of this common threats is based on the Governments 10 Steps to Cyber Security. Secondly the scheme can also function as an assurance framework, that will offers allow organisations the ability to demonstrate to their customers, investors, insurers and others that they have taken these essential precautions. Cyber Essentials offers a solid foundation for businesses to develop their cyber security controls measures that all types of organisations can implement and potentially build upon. It is important to reflex that although implementing the these measures can significantly reduce an organisation's vulnerability it will not remove all cyber security risk. Ultimately Cyber Essentials offers a focused set of controls that can provide organisations with a cost effective approach to limit the risks their organisation faces.

section: Which controls does Cyber Essentials cover?
text: Cyber Essentials covers five key controls: Boundary firewalls and internet gateways â prevention of unauthorized access Secure configuration â ensures secure system configuration Access Control â ensures appropriate access to systems Malware protection â installation and maintenance of virus and malware protection Patch management â application of patches and ensuring the latest version of applications is used

For third parties seeking to offer services within the Cloud Based Payments ecosystem the following compliance requirements are applicable: As a Cloud Based Payment Platform (CBPP) provider you must comply with the following requirements: Visa Cloud-Based Payments Program Minimum Requirements and Guidelines Visa Cloud-Based Payments Provider Security Requirements Mobile Application As a Mobile Application (MA) provider you must comply to the following requirements: Visa Cloud-Based Payments Contactless Specification Visa Cloud-Based Payments Program Minimum Requirements and Guidelines (functional & Security) Visa Brand Standards Mobile Application Platform As a Mobile Application Platform (MAP) provider you must comply with the following requirements: Visa Cloud-Based Payments Program Minimum Requirements and Guidelines PCI Data Security Standard (if the MAP transmits and or process cardholder information)

section: Host Based Emulation could help reclaim lost ground in digital payments
text: Cloud Based Payments offers the same contactless payment functionality on a mobile device that is currently provided by âVisa payWaveâ contactless cards, but develops this further by removing the need for a hardware secure element on the smartphone by allowing the secure deployment of accounts in the cloud. Cloud Based Payments allows cardholders to conduct their payment transactions in participating merchant locations using their smartphone with card emulation capabilities built outside of the secure element. Current examples of Host Based Emulation offered in the market are Androidâs Host Card Emulation and BlackBerryâs Software Card Emulation.

If you require your annual Cloud Payment audit or if you are interesting in consultancy regarding the secure operation of your Host based Card Emulation system please contact us using the contact form or give us a call on +44 (0) 8443 760400
The application installed on the smartphone to allow the management of enrolment, provisioning, lifecycle management and payment by the consumer.
The platform to provision and manage accounts according to issuer preferences.
The platform to securely transmit messages for enrolment, provisioning, active account management between the CBPP and Mobile Application.

The inception of Cloud Based Payments is seen as the future of card issuance where the emphasis is placed upon digital cards rather than the physical plastic cards that we are familiar with and use daily. In recent years NFC-enabled mobile phones have seen a transformation of the consumer shopping experience allowing consumers to pay for their goods and services using their smartphone and digital wallet. However the marketplace has been slow to adopt this technology, largely due to the complexity and size of the ecosystem.

The current Cloud Based Payments program offered by Visa aims to support payments across various channels and technologies. The Cloud Based Payments program consists of a number of functional components that support 'Visa payWave' payments using Host Card Emulation: Cloud Based Payments Platform The platform to provision and manage accounts according to issuer preferences. Mobile Application The application installed on the smartphone to allow the management of enrolment, provisioning, lifecycle management and payment by the consumer. Mobile Application Platform The platform to securely transmit messages for enrolment, provisioning, active account management between the CBPP and Mobile Application.

ISO27001 has become the de-facto standard for implementing an Information Security Management System. It is designed to provide a framework for implementation of technical and procedural security controls and processes for managing them. The framework implements the Plan, Do, Check, Act (PDCA) four-step management cycle methodology. A successful implementation sees the process go through the steps iteratively to achieve continual improvement.

Penetration testing focuses on exploiting system vulnerabilities. This is usually performed by a human using the same techniques currently being used by malicious threats in an attempt to breach your current security controls and gain access to your systems. Getting the test right As with any project, planning is the key to a successful delivery. When deciding how best to perform testing, clear objectives and goals must be defined and a framework developed to ensure those goals are reached. During the planning phase we will work with you to: Define your assets Define the deliverables Define the testing type required Define the rules of engagement Establish the lines of communication Once these have been completed we will create a test plan that will be reviewed and signed off before any testing takes place. Services There are a number of different test types that we can complete and these differ on the amount of prior knowledge the tester knows about the target and the amount of awareness the target has about the test. We have both experienced and professionally accredited penetration and vulnerability scanning professionals who can provide a range of services including: External Vulnerability Scanning (from a remote location) Internal Vulnerability Scanning (from the target location) External Network Penetration Testing (from a remote location) Internal Network Scanning (from the target location) Web Application testing Wireless Security testing

If you want to stay in business it is now critical for organisations to ensure that they are secure. Penetration Testing and Vulnerability Scanning is considered the best point in time assessment of your current security standing. Whilst Penetration Testing and Vulnerability Scanning get categorised together there are some slight differences.

In addition to the Formal Security Assessment described above, we will complete a more comprehensive assessment. We will look into the technical, physical and administrative controls to a much greater depth. We will also complete some Social Engineering techniques and complete a review of your Incident Management and Business Continuity plans.

The assessment will take place at the designated site and will be completed against a predefined set of standards. This may be an industry standard or one defined by our expert security consultants. This can include physical or logical security and is completed explicitly with full staff awareness. On completion of the assessment, AC Assure will provide a both a verbal debrief and a formal report recording any findings and any required remediation actions.

Using industry recognised tools and methodologies, our team of security professionals will perform a network vulnerability scan to evaluate the perimeter of your IT networks and provide you with an executive report on your overall security status.
If you would like to discuss cyber security for your business please contact us using the contact form or give us a call on +44 (0) 8443 760400

section: If your IT network is not secure can you afford to take the risk?
text: From a cost-benefit perspective, businesses that suffered a breach during the last year spent on average less of their IT budget on security than those that did not, with remediation actions after the breach forming the largest part of this spending. This suggests that businesses who have invested more in security defences have fewer breaches. Therefore given the rising costs of these breaches, under investment in security seems to be a false economy.

As well as Vulnerability Scanning and Penetration Testing, AC Assure can also provide a more comprehensive review of your technical controls. We can assess your patching and update system, backup systems, server and workstation configurations and also your development processes. We can also assess your policies and procedures to ensure your not only secure now, but have the process in place to safeguard your security in the future.

We will complete a full on-site review of the physical security of your organisation. This can include such tasks as reviewing your CCTV monitoring, review of your security guard configuration, evaluation of your pre-employment background checks and verifying your physical building security such as door and safe lock types.

Integration with other parties always includes risk. Indeed, some of the more well known security exploits of larger corporations have come from vulnerabilities in 3rd parties. To give you piece of mind and the confidence to trust another organization before you commit, AC Assure can complete a security assessment tailored to the particular integration. Having expertise in the security field, AC Assure can focus the review to the parts of the 3rd party holding your data and any extra exposure created by the business connection.

In the face of constantly changing technology, it is becoming increasingly difficult for businesses to protect their key information assets and IT infrastructure. If your business stores personal data you may be a target from outsider attacks and have a legal obligation to ensure that this data is stored and managed securely. â33% of small business were attacked by an unauthorised outsider in the past yearâ The 2014 IT security survey conducted by PWC and commissioned by the Department for Business Innovation & Skills found that 33% of small business were attacked by an unauthorised outsider in the past year. More details on the 2014 Information Security breaches Survey report conducted by PWC can be found here Although the number of security breaches affecting UK businesses has slightly decreased overall to 81% for large businesses and 60% for small businesses from 2013, the scale and cost of individual breaches has nearly doubled for small businesses with the average cost between £65k â £115k for their worst security breach incident. It is now apparent that cyber threats are a real risk for all businesses.

Being a proven security consultancy provider we have the skills and experience to asses you current security level. We can provide various packages from a high level office based risk assessment to a comprehensive technical and managerial review to suit both your size and budget. Once complete, we will provide you with a full report documenting the findings using a rating scale to suite and defining any remediation requirements. The following services we offer have been tailored from our extensive experience in this field:
